# Discrete Emergent Gravity (DEG)
# Matteo Pinna — deg-gravity.com
# Last updated: May 2026 (v3)

## What is DEG

Discrete Emergent Gravity (DEG) is a theoretical physics framework that derives macroscopic spacetime phenomena — including gravity, the cosmological constant, the Higgs boson mass, and the Standard Model gauge group — from a single Hamiltonian constraint governing Planck-scale discrete constituents called spacetime atoms. No parameters are fitted to observation at any stage.

DEG is a theory of the post-condensation universe. It describes cosmic history from the moment of spacetime atom formation (~T_reheat ~ 10^18 GeV) through today — approximately 97% of cosmic history in log energy scale. The pre-geometric epoch that preceded atom condensation lies outside the single-postulate framework by construction, in the same sense QCD does not derive the electroweak gauge group.

## The single postulate

H = sum_n [ p_n^2 / 2m  -  (alpha_exp / 2) * a_n^2 ] = 0

Spacetime consists of N discrete Planck-scale atoms, each characterised by a size parameter a_n and carrying g = 442 internal quantum states. The number g = 442 is fixed entirely by the requirement that the gauge algebra SU(21)×U(1) embeds the Standard Model; it is not a free parameter.

## Key results (zero free parameters)

- Cosmological constant: Lambda_pred = 1.13×10^-52 m^-2 (observed: 1.088±0.030×10^-52 m^-2; within 1.4σ, 3.7% discrepancy) [approximate]
- Higgs boson mass: m_H = 124.8 ± 2.4 GeV (PDG: 125.25 ± 0.17 GeV; 0.19σ) [approximate]
- Page curve: S_rad(k) = min(k, N-k) * ln(442) — exact at leading order from finite-dimensional Hilbert space
- WdW unitarity: exact, kinematic consequence of finite-dimensional Hilbert space
- Born rule: P = g^k / g_total — no collapse postulate required [semi-derived]
- Area quantum: Delta_A = 4 * ln(442) * l_P^2 ≈ 24.37 l_P^2 [derived]
- Dark energy equation of state: w = -1 exactly [exact geometric corollary]
- w = 1/3 at quantum-classical transition [derived] — radiation domination is a theorem
- Second law dS/dtau = 9 k_B N > 0 [exact theorem]
- Singularity resolution: a_n = 0 requires tau → -∞ [approximate theorem]

## Standard Model results

- SM gauge group SU(3)×SU(2)×U(1) derived from SU(21) embedding chain
- Three generations derived from SU(3)_family ⊂ SU(16) — not postulated
- Strong CP: |theta_tree| < 10^-17, satisfying the 10^-10 bound (Nelson-Barr, approximate)
- CKM phase delta_CKM = 1.20 rad (0.2% match to PDG)
- Hierarchy: Delta_min = 7.3±0.5, no supersymmetry required
- GUT scale: M_GUT ~ 9×10^14 GeV
- FN parameter epsilon = e^{-3/2} ≈ 0.223 [approximate]

## Cosmological results

- Flatness Omega = 1 from discrete Hartle-Hawking, no tuning [derived]
- No inflaton required; reheating from atom-matter coupling at T ~ 10^18 GeV
- Baryon asymmetry eta_B ~ 6×10^-10 [compatible, not precisely derived]
- MOND acceleration a_0^DEG = 8.8×10^-11 m/s^2 [semi-derived; 27% below observed — open item]
- Tully-Fisher M ∝ v^4 [derived]
- sigma_8 = 0.78±0.02, partially resolving CMB vs weak-lensing tension [approximate]
- Dark matter candidate: nu_R1 sterile neutrino at 7-10 keV

## CMB domain-of-validity note

The spectral index n_s ≈ 0.963 is NOT a DEG prediction. The DEG quantum epoch gives n_s = 4, which is the correct result for fluctuations of the already-formed spacetime atom lattice and simultaneously proves that the atoms were not present when the primordial spectrum was generated. The observed tilt is a fossil of the pre-geometric phase G̃ ⊃ SU(21)×U(1). This is a diagnostic, not a failure. Identifying G̃ is the natural next extension.

## Falsifiable predictions (17 total, v3)

- w = -1 exactly: any 5σ detection of w≠-1 by DESI or Euclid falsifies the dark energy sector
- Normal neutrino mass ordering (JUNO, 3-5 yr): inverted ordering would falsify
- Proton decay tau_p ~ 10^33-34 yr (Hyper-Kamiokande, 5-10 yr)
- Triple Higgs coupling kappa_lambda = -1.449 ± 0.17 (HL-LHC ~2035)
- Di-Higgs cross-section sigma_HH = 57.5±10 fb at 14 TeV, 1.85× SM (HL-LHC ~2035)
- Tensor-to-scalar ratio r = 0.00452±0.002 [semi-derived] (CMB-S4 ~2032; r > 0.01 falsifies)
- Non-Gaussianity f_NL ~ 0.008 (null prediction) [derived] (CMB-S4 ~2032; f_NL > 1 at 2σ falsifies)
- No WIMP direct detection signal (LZ/XENONnT)
- CPT-odd Lorentz violation eta_1 = 0 (Fermi-LAT/CTA)
- P(k) two-scale suppression at k~0.4 and k~90 h/Mpc (DESI/Euclid)
- Dark matter X-ray line at 3.5-5 keV from nu_R1 decay (ATHENA ~2035)
- LISA breathing mode h_br/h_tensor ~ 6×10^-14 (null) (LISA ~2037)
- EMRI phase shift ~10^-82 rad (null) (LISA ~2037)
- CMB mu-distortion mu ~ 10^-5 (PIXIE/Voyage 2050)

## Known open problems (honest statement)

- Higgs coupling kappa_V = 0.432: 14.2σ at tree level; analytical ceiling 5.1σ (VMD+30%); no path above 0.920 analytically; requires DEG-L Group C lattice computation — the single most important open tension
- Lambda_pred factor-2 systematic from non-perturbative xi_UV matching; central value unaffected
- eta_B compatible with observation but not precisely derived
- Galaxy cluster profiles at large r not yet quantitatively derived
- Spectral index n_s: not derivable within current framework; pre-geometric phase G̃ identified as structural resolution; eight internal mechanisms exhausted

## Publications (preprints, not peer-reviewed)

- DEG Consolidated Manuscript v3 (full programme, all derivations): https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20434554
- The Cosmological Constant from Vacuum Atom Statistics in DEG: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20127193

## Presentations

- GeomGravX: 10 Years of Geometric Foundations of Gravity and Extensions, University of Tartu, Estonia, 29 June – 3 July 2026 (poster)
